mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market demand and future consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 149750.0 thousand, near historical highs
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) continues rising, the real policy rate becomes restrictive, signaling monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS*CPIAUCSL interaction term and forward returns is < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,100 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as construction and materials sectors contract.
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction or rising rate environment
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where HOUST < 1100, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 4.0%, indicating simultaneous supply weakness and labor market softening that reduces corporate earnings and drives negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure or post-shock recovery failure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO deceleration × UNRATE rise) shows Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.8% month-over-month, signaling robust consumer demand and forward earnings growth that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, particularly in consumer discretionary and technology sectors.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 56% of instances where RSAFS growth exceeds 0.8%, or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 1.5% annualized while the federal funds rate remains above 2.0%, indicating monetary policy is restrictive relative to economic slack, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18671.497 billion chained 2017 dollars, FEDFUNDS at 2.19%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 62% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the GDPC1-FEDFUNDS interaction < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth turns negative, signaling labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0
regime_hint: labor market inflection or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 64% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the UNRATE-PAYEMS interaction < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expands above 0.6% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable (month-over-month change < 0.3%), indicating healthy supply-side growth without inflationary pressure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through margin expansion.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.6% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.3%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the INDPRO-CPIAUCSL interaction < 0.035 over any 3-year rolling window.
